centralize.title: We centralize what's scattered

about.title: A systems studio, not a software house
about.subtitle: We architect solutions across software and IoT — partnering with businesses to rebuild how they operate.

insight.title: The insight we started with
insight.text: Trixync was founded on a simple observation: most businesses don't need "an app." They need their actual workflow rebuilt so technology disappears into the background.

problem.title: The problem is rarely the screen
problem.text: Whether it's attendance collected on paper, memberships tracked across spreadsheets, or bookings reconciled between phone calls and a calendar — the bottleneck is almost always the process, not the interface.

build.title: How we build from there
build.text: We sit with the people who run the operation, map what's actually happening, and design systems that fit how the business works today — while being built to scale for tomorrow.

values.title: What drives our work
values.subtitle: Three commitments that show up in every engagement — from discovery through years after launch.

production.title: Production-first mindset
production.description: We invest in caching, access control, payment integration, and async processing — the unglamorous work that keeps systems alive.

collaboration.title: Stakeholder collaboration
collaboration.description: We build with the people who'll use the system, not from specs handed down secondhand.

partnership.title: Long-term partnership
partnership.description: Launch is a milestone, not an exit. We stay involved as your business and systems evolve.

location.title: Rooted in Medan
location.description: Headquartered in Medan, North Sumatra — working with clients across Indonesia and open to remote collaboration.

cta.title: Let's map your workflow
cta.description: Whether you're in Sumatra or collaborating remotely, we'll start by understanding how your operation actually runs.

services.title: What we build
services.subtitle: End-to-end systems engineering — from workflow discovery to production deployment and beyond.

process.title: How we work with you
process.subtitle: Every engagement follows the same discipline — mapped to your operations, not a generic dev cycle.

discover.title: Discover the workflow
discover.description: We sit with the people who run the operation and map what's actually happening — not what's assumed.

design.title: Design for adoption
design.description: Systems shaped around how your team works today, with room to scale tomorrow.

deliver.title: Ship to production

support.title: Stay after launch

cta.title: Not sure which service fits?
cta.description: Tell us what's manual, duplicated, or held together with spreadsheets. We'll map the bottleneck and recommend honestly.

workflow-systems.title: Custom Business Systems
workflow-systems.problem: Manual processes, disconnected tools, and workarounds that don't scale.
workflow-systems.build: Tailored platforms that digitize your actual workflow — bookings, operations, reporting, and admin.
workflow-systems.outcome: One system your team actually uses, replacing scattered spreadsheets and ad-hoc tools.

iot-integration.title: IoT & Hardware Integration
iot-integration.problem: Software that can't reach the physical world — attendance devices, sensors, offline environments.
iot-integration.build: Offline-first integrations with fingerprint scanners, IoT hardware, and edge devices.
iot-integration.outcome: Reliable data flow between the field and your central system, even without constant connectivity.

platform-centralization.title: Platform Centralization
platform-centralization.problem: Critical data spread across cities, departments, and communication channels.
platform-centralization.build: Unified membership, HR, scheduling, and supplier/reseller platforms with real-time tracking.
platform-centralization.outcome: A single source of truth that thousands of users across multiple locations can trust.

production-infrastructure.title: Production Infrastructure
production-infrastructure.problem: Systems that work in demos but fail under real traffic, roles, and payment flows.
production-infrastructure.build: RBAC, payment gateways, caching layers, async task queues, and monitoring.
production-infrastructure.outcome: Infrastructure that survives real usage — not a prototype dressed up for a presentation.

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Systems we've designed, built, and shipped — with the problem, approach, and solution behind each one.

caseStudy.title: Problem → approach → solution
caseStudy.subtitle: How we mapped the bottleneck, designed around real workflows, and shipped something that runs in production.

kms-connect.title: KMS Connect App
kms-connect.description: Fully integrated job listing system with mobile apps, connecting job opportunities and application workflows in one platform experience.
kms-connect.problem: Job listings and applications were scattered across channels — hard for candidates to discover roles and for recruiters to track progress in one place.
kms-connect.approach: We mapped the full hiring workflow first: listing creation, discovery, application, and status updates. Then designed one backend with dedicated mobile experiences for each side of the market.
kms-connect.solution: A Django API powering Flutter mobile apps for job seekers and recruiters — unified listings, applications, and notifications in a single platform.
kms-connect.outcome: One system of record for jobs and applications instead of spreadsheets, chat threads, and duplicate data entry.

gerindra-sumut.title: Gerindra Sumut Membership System
gerindra-sumut.description: Membership management system for Gerindra Sumut with secure member data, search, and reporting.
gerindra-sumut.problem: Member records lived in spreadsheets and paper — slow to search, hard to audit, and painful to scale across districts.
gerindra-sumut.approach: We worked with administrators to define how members are registered, verified, and reported on — including document intake that couldn't stay manual forever.
gerindra-sumut.solution: A containerized Django platform with structured member profiles, search, reporting, and OCR-assisted document processing for faster verification.
gerindra-sumut.outcome: Secure, searchable membership data with reporting leadership can trust — replacing ad-hoc files and manual reconciliation.

yanks-brits.title: Yanks & Brits Employee System
yanks-brits.description: Employee management system handling scheduling, payroll, and staff data for Yanks & Brits.
yanks-brits.problem: Schedules, attendance, and payroll depended on manual rosters and back-office spreadsheets — error-prone and slow every pay cycle.
yanks-brits.approach: We sat with ops staff to understand how shifts are planned, swapped, and paid — then built around those steps instead of a generic HR template.
yanks-brits.solution: A Django employee platform covering scheduling, staff records, and payroll workflows in one place with role-based access.
yanks-brits.outcome: Fewer payroll mistakes and less time reconciling rosters — staff data and schedules stay aligned in one system.

goholiday-dc.title: GoHoliday DC
goholiday-dc.description: Travel marketplace platform where travel businesses can sell tour packages, and resellers can promote tours and earn commission from successful sales.
goholiday-dc.problem: Tour inventory, reseller promotion, and commission tracking were disconnected — sales happened in chats and spreadsheets with no shared source of truth.
goholiday-dc.approach: We designed a marketplace model: suppliers publish packages, resellers promote with trackable links, and commissions calculate from actual completed sales.
goholiday-dc.solution: A Next.js front office with a Django backend — catalog, reseller dashboards, order flow, and automated commission tracking in one platform.
goholiday-dc.outcome: Suppliers and resellers operate from the same system — transparent commissions and less manual reconciliation after every campaign.

pgi-tower.title: PGI Tower
pgi-tower.description: Landing page website for PGI Tower that presents the property profile, facilities, and contact conversion points with a modern responsive layout.
pgi-tower.problem: The property needed a credible web presence — brochure PDFs and outdated pages weren't converting inquiries or presenting facilities clearly.
pgi-tower.approach: We structured the site around how prospects evaluate property: location, facilities, unit types, and clear contact paths — mobile-first.
pgi-tower.solution: A fast Next.js marketing site with Tailwind CSS — responsive layouts, facility highlights, and conversion-focused contact sections.
pgi-tower.outcome: A modern property profile prospects can browse on any device — with clear paths to inquiry instead of scattered offline follow-up.

sejahtera-bersama-erp.title: Sejahtera Bersama ERP
sejahtera-bersama-erp.description: End-to-end ERP for UD Sejahtera Bersama — inventory, attendance, payroll, purchasing, POS, and production workflow in one system.
sejahtera-bersama-erp.problem: Stock, purchasing, attendance, payroll, and production lived in separate spreadsheets — no live stock health, no reliable HPP (cost of goods), and constant manual reconciliation.
sejahtera-bersama-erp.approach: We mapped the full operational loop — from purchase orders and goods receiving to production, sales, and payroll — then designed one ERP where each module feeds the next instead of duplicate data entry.
sejahtera-bersama-erp.solution: A Django ERP covering inventory management, attendance, payroll, purchase orders, goods buying, automated HPP calculation, POS, and a production workflow — with a live operations dashboard.
sejahtera-bersama-erp.outcome: One source of truth for stock, cost, and staff — real-time stock health and accurate HPP replacing scattered spreadsheets.

itnb-portal.title: IT&B Campus Portal
itnb-portal.description: Institutional portal for Institut Bisnis IT&B connecting students, lecturers, and alumni — digital ID cards, GPA, announcements, and webinar registration.
itnb-portal.problem: Student records, campus news, GPA lookups, and event sign-ups were spread across manual processes — no single place for students, lecturers, and alumni to access what they needed.
itnb-portal.approach: We designed role-based journeys for students, lecturers, and alumni, then centralized the records and communications each group depends on into one portal.
itnb-portal.solution: A Django portal issuing digital ID cards, showing GPA and academic records, publishing campus announcements, and running smart webinar registration for students, lecturers, and alumni.
itnb-portal.outcome: One campus portal for identity, grades, news, and events — replacing scattered manual lookups across the institution.

chinergy-shenindo.title: CV Chinergy Shenindo
chinergy-shenindo.description: Corporate landing page for an industrial solutions company — spare parts, machinery repair, fabrication, and equipment rental across Indonesia.
chinergy-shenindo.problem: An established industrial supplier had no credible web presence to showcase its service lines, catalog depth, and nationwide reach to prospective plantation and industrial clients.
chinergy-shenindo.approach: We structured the site around how industrial buyers evaluate a supplier — services, spare parts catalog, partner network, and clear quotation paths — in both English and Indonesian.
chinergy-shenindo.solution: A multilingual Next.js marketing site with Tailwind CSS presenting fabrication, repair, and spare parts offerings, partner regions, and conversion-focused contact and quotation sections.
chinergy-shenindo.outcome: A trusted online profile that presents Chinergy's full industrial capability and drives quotation requests instead of offline-only outreach.

citikopi.title: Citikopi Mandailing Beans
citikopi.description: Multilingual brand and export landing page for a Mandailing coffee and Indonesian specialty producer — from single-origin beans to global markets.
citikopi.problem: A specialty coffee and spice exporter needed to present its Mandailing heritage, ethical sourcing story, and product range credibly to international buyers.
citikopi.approach: We built the narrative around origin and craft — highlands, single-origin beans, sustainable sourcing — with clear paths to products and export partnership inquiries.
citikopi.solution: A polished multilingual Next.js site with Tailwind CSS showcasing products, sourcing values, brand story, and partner/export contact points.
citikopi.outcome: An export-ready brand presence that tells Citikopi's origin story and opens conversations with global buyers.

genba-solusindo.title: PT Genba Inti Solusindo
genba-solusindo.description: Landing page for an AC & HVAC contractor in Medan — product lineup, services, and lead capture for commercial and residential clients.
genba-solusindo.problem: An AC and HVAC contractor needed a professional web presence to present its product lineup, installation and maintenance services, and capture inquiries across Sumatra.
genba-solusindo.approach: We organized the site around what buyers look for — AC types, services, and trust signals — with clear contact and inquiry paths.
genba-solusindo.solution: A responsive Next.js landing page with Tailwind CSS presenting the AC product lineup, HVAC services, company profile, and conversion-focused contact sections.
genba-solusindo.outcome: A credible contractor profile that showcases the product range and turns visitors into service inquiries.

skyview-setiabudi.title: Skyview Setiabudi Apartment
skyview-setiabudi.description: Property landing page for a modern apartment in central Medan — facilities, unit types, pricing, and booking conversion.
skyview-setiabudi.problem: A modern apartment development needed a compelling web presence to present its facilities, unit types, and pricing, and to funnel prospects toward official booking.
skyview-setiabudi.approach: We structured the site the way buyers evaluate a residence — location, facilities, unit types with pricing, and gallery — with clear, trusted booking paths.
skyview-setiabudi.solution: A responsive Next.js site with Tailwind CSS highlighting premium facilities, studio-to-2BR unit types with pricing, photo gallery, and official contact and booking sections.
skyview-setiabudi.outcome: A polished property profile that presents units and facilities clearly and channels inquiries to official booking rather than scattered offline follow-up.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Straight answers about how we work — process, timelines, IoT, and what happens after launch.

cta.title: Ask us directly
cta.description: If your situation doesn't fit a FAQ answer, tell us what's going on. We'll respond within one business day.

what-is-trixync.question: What is Trixync?
what-is-trixync.answer: Trixync is a technology and systems studio based in Medan, North Sumatra. We architect custom software and IoT solutions that rebuild how businesses operate — not just add another app to the stack.

engagement-process.question: How does your engagement process work?
engagement-process.answer: We start by understanding your actual workflow and bottlenecks — sitting with your team, not just reviewing a brief. From there we design, build in production-grade increments, and stay involved after launch.

timeline.question: How long does a typical project take?
timeline.answer: Timelines depend on scope and complexity. A focused workflow system might take 6–10 weeks; a multi-module platform with IoT integration can take 3–6 months. We'll give you a realistic estimate after discovery.

pricing.question: How do you price projects?
pricing.answer: We scope projects based on the problem being solved, not hours logged. After discovery, we provide a clear proposal with milestones — no surprise invoices.

iot-capability.question: Can you integrate with hardware and IoT devices?
iot-capability.answer: Yes. We've built offline-first systems with fingerprint scanners, sensors, and edge devices. We're comfortable where software meets the physical world.

post-launch.question: Do you provide support after launch?
post-launch.answer: Absolutely. Launch is a milestone, not an exit. We offer ongoing maintenance, feature evolution, and infrastructure monitoring as your business grows.

industries.question: What industries do you work with?
industries.answer: We've worked with government agencies, HR teams, tour operators, membership organizations, and distribution networks. Our focus is operational complexity, not a specific vertical.

remote-work.question: Do you work with remote clients?
remote-work.answer: Yes. While we're based in Medan, we collaborate with teams across Indonesia and internationally via structured remote workflows.

medan-based.question: Are you only available in Medan?
medan-based.answer: We're headquartered in Medan, North Sumatra, but serve clients nationwide. For local businesses in Sumatra, we offer on-site discovery sessions.

maintenance.question: Can you take over an existing system?
maintenance.answer: In many cases, yes. We'll assess the codebase, infrastructure, and operational fit before recommending whether to extend, refactor, or rebuild.

contact.title: Let's talk about your workflow
contact.subtitle: Tell us what's manual, scattered, or breaking under load. We'll respond within one business day.

channels.title: Direct channels
channels.description: Prefer a quick message? Use the form or reach us directly — whichever fits your workflow.

response.title: One business day
response.description: We read every message personally and reply with next steps — not an auto-responder.

review.title: We review your context
review.description: Project type, budget range, and the workflow challenge you described.

respond.title: We reply with clarity
respond.description: A direct response within one business day — questions, fit, or a call suggestion.

discover.title: Discovery if it's a fit
discover.description: We map the bottleneck together before proposing scope, timeline, or architecture.

form.budgetHint: Approximate range is fine — helps us suggest the right scope.

whatsappMessage.intro: Hi Trixync! New project inquiry from your website:

footer.description: We map how your business runs — then build production systems across software and IoT.
footer.ctaTitle: Tell us what your team still does by hand.
footer.ctaDescription: The repetitive, the duplicated, the spreadsheet-and-WhatsApp workarounds — walk us through them. We'll pinpoint the real bottleneck and tell you straight whether a system is worth building.
